page: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-96xl is a memory-intensive bare-metal server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features dedicated Intel processors operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, delivering 192 physical cores and 384 virtual CPUs via hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 6144.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per physical core.

This server type does not include local storage, requiring external storage solutions for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps. There are no integrated graphics processing units (GPUs) or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

Given the massive memory capacity and high core count, this bare-metal instance offers high resource density for memory-bound enterprise workloads. It is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, massive data analytics, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and heavy virtualization workloads that require substantial RAM without the need for local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.metal-96xl is a memory-intensive bare-metal server utilizing the x86_64 architecture with dedicated Intel processors. Operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, it provides 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs alongside 6144.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. The server features a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage, GPU accelerators, and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external storage solutions for data persistence. This configuration is engineered for large-scale memory-bound workloads, such as in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and extensive virtualization environments that require massive memory footprints without local storage dependencies.
